msgs.text:
Turn 10 ‚Äî degree 16 should be treated as a quadratic torsor, not as one tail I used only the files attached to this message. The target remains the over-G isomorphism problem for the two quotients defined by the relators rS,rH, with canonical projections to G and the required condition œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.The attached history has already reduced the surviving obstruction problem to the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1, section-twisted Magnus equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚àí1Y1‚àí2Y03Y0q with target X2=1+A2, and Turn 9 cleared the tower throughS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI16).

Turn 9 also correctly identified the next layer as degree 16, where the quotient isL16/im‚àÇ15‚âÖL8[2],dimL8=36,and where 8+8 correction products first enter.The main conclusion of this turn is more structural than numerical:degree¬Ý16¬Ýis¬Ýnot¬Ýrepresented¬Ýby¬Ýa¬Ýwell-defined¬Ýfixed-gauge¬Ýtail.

The correct object is a quadratic affine torsorŒò16:kerD‚â§15‚ü∂L16/im‚àÇ15‚âÖL8[2],not a single homogeneous degree-16 word computed from one arbitrarily chosen lift.Here is the clean formulation.LetL‚àô‚äÇF2‚ü®‚ü®A,B‚ü©‚ü©be the free restricted Lie algebra, and let‚àÇn:Ln‚äïLn‚ÜíLn+1,(W,Q)‚Ü¶[A,W]+[B,Q],with [U,V]=UV+VU in characteristic 2. The earlier turns established the pattern that odd layers are formal boundaries, while even layers have the restricted-square quotient Lm[2]; this was used at degrees 10,12,14.

At degree 16, the relevant dimensions aredimL8=36,dimL9=56,dimL10=105,dimL11=186,dimL12=346,dimL13=630,dimL14=1179,dimL15=2182,dimL16=4116.Thus rank‚àÇ15=4116‚àí36=4080,and the cokernel is exactly the 36-dimensional square quotientL16/im‚àÇ15‚âÖL8[2].Now take the full high-correction space preserving the already-built I9 base lift:V‚â§14=d=8‚®Å14(Ld‚äïLd),where the two copies correspond to corrections placed on Y1 and on the section twist q. Its dimension isdimV‚â§14=2(36+56+105+186+346+630+1179)=5076.The lower target degrees 9,‚Ä¶,15 have total restricted-Lie dimension 56+105+186+346+630+1179+2182=4684.Because Turn 9 already had full rank through degree 14, and degree 15 is an odd formal-boundary layer, the lower-tail mapD‚â§15:V‚â§14‚ÜíT9‚äï‚ãØ‚äïT15 has full target rank 4684. Therefore dimkerD‚â§15=5076‚àí4684=392.So the degree-16 problem is not small in the sense of ‚Äúlook at one tail.‚Äù There are 392 invisible high-correction directions that preserve every previously cleared layer.

For k‚ààkerD‚â§15, changing the lift by k does not affect degrees 9,‚Ä¶,15, but it can change the degree-16 square class byŒò16 (k)=‚Ñì16 (k)+Q8,8(k).

Here ‚Ñì16 is the linear forced-Bockstein contribution of the actual restricted representatives, while Q8,8 is the first genuinely nonlinear Maurer‚ÄìCartan term coming from products of degree-8 corrections. This is exactly the interaction Turn 9 warned would first appear at degree 16.

So the precise degree-16 criterion is:degree¬Ý16¬Ýdies¬Ýiff¬Ýthe¬Ýcurrent¬Ýsquare¬Ýclass¬Ýin¬ÝL8[2]lies¬Ýin¬ÝimŒò16.

A fixed solution through I16 may display a nonzero degree-16 square class, but that class is not invariant unless it is first quotiented by the whole Œò16 -action. This is the same structural lesson as degrees 6,8,10,12,14: single-gauge tails have repeatedly looked dangerous and then disappeared after the correct affine freedom was included. The attached history explicitly records this phenomenon at degree 12, where the na√Øve ker‚àÇ10 test saw only rank 9 of an 11-dimensional square quotient, while the full affine kernel supplied the missing directions.

The likely uniform induction statement is now visible:Pm:kerD‚â§2m‚àí1‚ü∂L2m/im‚àÇ2m‚àí1‚âÖLm[2]should be the relevant obstruction-moving map at every even layer 2m. The prior turns verify the analogue for m=5,6,7, i.e. degrees 10,12,14, with the important refinement that the domain must be the full affine kernel, not just the most recent ker‚àÇ.I am not going to claim in this turn that degree 16 is cleared. The honest result is that the correct degree-16 obstruction has now been isolated as a 36-dimensional quadratic image problem with a 392-dimensional source. Proving surjectivity of Œò16, or at least membership of the actual degree-16 square class in its image, is the next decisive test. If that succeeds, the odd-layer argument immediately clears degree 17 as well, just as before.

ESTABLISHED: The degree-16 obstruction is not a fixed-gauge tail. It is the class in L16/im‚àÇ15‚âÖL8[2], acted on by a quadratic affine map Œò16:kerD‚â§15‚ÜíL8[2], where dimkerD‚â§15=392 and dimL8[2]=36.

GAP: I have not proved that Œò16 hits the actual degree-16 class, nor that it is surjective. Therefore this turn does not yet prove ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

NEXT: Compute the rank/image of the quadratic affine map Œò16, including both the linear Bockstein part and the 8+8 Maurer‚ÄìCartan polarization. If imŒò16=L8[2], degree 16 dies uniformly and degree 17 follows formally.[DEG16-QUADRATIC-TORSOR-ISOLATED]
